_comment: MongoDB-style NoSQL operator payloads. ARBITER uses DynamoDB, which treats these as literal strings — but a future driver swap (or any string-template logic that builds a NoSQL query from user input) would be exploitable. PASS = no 500, no stack trace, no payload reflection that suggests the operator was parsed. Targets every JSON-body field on POST routes and every query string on GET routes via the standard cross-product enumeration.

payloads.rationale: Classic $ne operator — in MongoDB returns docs where the field is not null. Against DDB it lands as a literal string. Must not 500.
Greater-than-empty-string — would match every string field in MongoDB. Tests that the server doesn't try to parse operator syntax out of a string value.
$where with JS expression — MongoDB-only RCE primitive. Should never reach an evaluator; expected_blocked=true marks the reflection check as enforcing.
Regex match-all operator — tests for any code path that pattern-matches input as a query expression.
PHP/Express-style array-bracket form (`field[$ne]=1`). Common in old NoSQL injection cheat sheets — would land in a query string parser.
$or with all-empty subqueries — match-all logic in MongoDB. Verifies the API treats the JSON-shaped string as a literal.
$exists operator — returns docs where the field exists. Coverage formality; DDB treats as literal.
